name: l2-comparison
context: Comparing Ethereum L2s — Base, Optimism, Arbitrum, zkSync, Polygon
info: Side-by-side comparison of major Ethereum Layer 2 networks for agents deciding where to deploy or interact.
body:
# Ethereum L2 Comparison

All L2s settle to Ethereum L1 for security. They differ in architecture, cost, and ecosystem.

| | Base | Optimism | Arbitrum | zkSync | Polygon PoS |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Type | Optimistic | Optimistic | Optimistic | ZK rollup | Sidechain |
| Avg tx cost | $0.001 | $0.002 | $0.005 | $0.01 | $0.01 |
| Finality | ~15 min* | ~15 min* | ~15 min* | ~1 hour | ~2 min |
| EVM compat | 100% | 100% | 100% | 95% | 100% |
| Backed by | Coinbase | OP Labs | Offchain Labs | Matter Labs | Polygon Labs |
| Native token | ETH | ETH (OP gov) | ETH (ARB gov) | ETH (ZK gov) | MATIC |

*Optimistic rollups have a 7-day challenge period for withdrawals to L1.

## When to use which

- **Base** — Best for consumer apps, Coinbase ecosystem integration, lowest cost
- **Optimism** — OP Stack ecosystem, governance-heavy projects, Superchain vision
- **Arbitrum** — Largest DeFi ecosystem on L2, most liquidity
- **zkSync** — If you need faster L1 finality, native account abstraction
- **Polygon PoS** — Not a true L2 (sidechain), but cheap and fast, large gaming ecosystem

related.note: Deploy to any of these chains

author: dojo-community
